nodes.context: Yakuza group based in Hiroshima, Japan; second largest in Chugoku region after Kyosei-kai.
Yakuza group in Chugoku region; largest in the region.
Bakuto organization formed after WWII in Onomichi, Hiroshima; predecessor of Kyodo-kai.
Anti-Yamaguchi federation including Kyodo-kai, Kyosei-kai, Asano-gumi, Goda-ikka, and Shinwa-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: the second largest yakuza group in the Chugoku region after the Kyosei-kai
edges.context: Kyodo-kai is smaller than Kyosei-kai in the Chugoku region.

edges.evidence_quote: Kokichi Morita, then the highest-ranked senior member of the Takahashi-gumi, formed the Kyodo-kai in January 1969 following the Takahashi-gumi's disbanding.
edges.context: Kyodo-kai was formed by a senior member of Takahashi-gumi after its disbanding.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai
edges.context: Kyodo-kai is a member of the Gosha-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai
edges.context: Kyodo-kai and Kyosei-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Asano-gumi
edges.context: Kyodo-kai and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Asano-gumi , the Goda-ikka
edges.context: Kyodo-kai and Goda-ikka are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Kyodo-kai has been a member of an anti-Yamaguchi federation named the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Asano-gumi , the Goda-ikka , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai
edges.context: Kyodo-kai and Shinwa-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
